title: Complete wallet creation: return credentials once, secure listWallets, improve error handling

requirements.summary: Update frontend error handling to display structured, user-friendly wallet creation error messages instead of generic 'Failed to create wallet' text
acceptanceCriteria: The `walletErrors.ts` utility maps every backend error variant (#randomnessError, #limitReached, #notAuthenticated, #internalError) to a specific, user-friendly message.
The `EpiqWalletsPanel` component displays the mapped error message and never shows 'Failed to create wallet' to the user.
All wallet creation failures show actionable, human-readable error text.

file_operations.description: Extend the error mapping logic to handle new structured backend error variants (#randomnessError, #limitReached, #notAuthenticated, #internalError) returned by createWallet. Map each variant to a specific, user-friendly message string (e.g., 'Unable to generate secure randomness', 'You have reached the 9-wallet limit', 'Authentication required to create a wallet', 'An internal error occurred').

Update the wallet creation error handling to use the enhanced walletErrors utility. When createWallet returns an error variant, pass it to the mapping function and display the resulting user-friendly message in the UI. Remove any fallback to 'Failed to create wallet'. Ensure error state is cleared when the user retries or closes the error display.

Update the createWallet mutation to properly extract and forward structured error variants from the backend WalletOperationResult_1 response type. Ensure the mutation error object includes the specific error variant (e.g., #randomnessError) so downstream components can map it to a user-friendly message.
